title: Quantifying Thiamin Cellular Quotas in Algae
author: Magdalena Gutowska
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.j6ncrde

steps.title: Maintain cultures of CCMP2090 Emiliania huxleyi...
steps.instruction: Maintain cultures of CCMP2090 Emiliania huxleyi in mid-exponential growth for ≥10generations using semi-continuous batch culture techniques. Grow cultures usingartificial seawater based medium L1-Si (Guillard 1975).

steps.title: Transfer cells daily (replete vitamin amendment...
steps.instruction: Transfer cells daily (replete vitamin amendment conditions) or every second day (limitedvitamin amendment conditions) to not exceed 1 x 106 cells mL-

steps.title: Quantify the cellconcentrations on a flow cytometer (e
steps.instruction: Quantify the cellconcentrations on a flow cytometer (e.g., Accuri C6 cytometer BD Biosciences, USA).

steps.title: Select the vitamin amendment conditions for...
steps.instruction: Select the vitamin amendment conditions for the experiment and set up triplicate culturesfor each treatment. For luxury treatment, supply thiamin at 300 nmol L-1 (standard f/2medium concentration). For replete conditions supply thiamin and HMP at 10 nmol L-1.For limiting conditions supply thiamin at 500 pmol L-1 and HMP at 100 pmol L-1.

steps.title: Maintain cells in mid-exponential growth for...
steps.instruction: Maintain cells in mid-exponential growth for ≥10 generations using semi-continuous batchculture techniques. Quantify the cell concentrations on a flow cytometer.

steps.title: Remove vitamin amendments from media by...
steps.instruction: Remove vitamin amendments from media by using centrifugation wash steps withthiamin-free medium. Centrifuge 100 mL of culture at 4000 x g for 10 min at 4oC. Removethe supernatant, add 50 mL of thiamin-free medium to wash cells and repeat (wash,centrifugation etc.) three times.

steps.title: After the last wash step, resuspend the pellet in 1
steps.instruction: After the last wash step, resuspend the pellet in 1.5 mL of thiamin-free medium andquantify the cell concentration on a flow cytometer.

steps.title: Generate a final cell pellet
steps.instruction: Generate a final cell pellet. Centrifuge at 10,000 x g for 30 min at 4oC. Collect thesupernatant. Remove as much of the supernatant as possible and retain in a separatetube. Flash-freeze the pellet in liquid nitrogen prior to storage at -80oC.

steps.title: Combine the removed supernatant aliquots and...
steps.instruction: Combine the removed supernatant aliquots and quantify the cell concentration in thesupernatant on a flow cytometer. Subtract these numbers (in the supernatant) from thetotal cell numbers determined prior to the final centrifugation step. This will give you thefinal cell count in the cell pellet.

steps.title: Monitor the chlorophyll fluorescence and FALS...
steps.instruction: Monitor the chlorophyll fluorescence and FALS distribution on the flow cytometrichistograms to make sure cells have remained intact during the washing andcentrifugation steps. See Figure below for representative cytograms that depict an intactcell population from a start culture, versus a cell population that has been resuspendedfrom a pellet. A low (<1%) number of ruptured cells is evident – and these cells andreleased thiamin would be removed with removal of the final supernatant (after final spin)– so it is unlikely the influence quotas. a

steps.title: Suspend pellet in 100 μL of...
steps.instruction: Suspend pellet in 100 μL of 7% HClO4 and sonicate for 2 min

steps.title: Add 50 μL 4 M CH3CO2K...
steps.instruction: Add 50 μL 4 M CH3CO2K and 50 μL 30 mg mL-1 K3[Fe(CN)6] in 7 M NaOH. Pipette tomix. Leave 1 min.

steps.title: Immediately neutralize the reaction with 6 M aqueous HCl
steps.instruction: Immediately neutralize the reaction with 6 M aqueous HCl.

steps.title: Centrifuge the sample and collect the supernatant for further analysis
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the sample and collect the supernatant for further analysis. a

steps.title: Purify the deionized water that is...
steps.instruction: Purify the deionized water that is use for reagents with active charcoal and filter.

steps.title: Use a reverse phase HPLC (Agilent...
steps.instruction: Use a reverse phase HPLC (Agilent 1200 series) with fluorescence detection. Set thedetector at an excitation of 365 nm and emission of 444 nm.

steps.title: Prepare a Supelcosil SPLC-18-DB (25 cm...
steps.instruction: Prepare a Supelcosil SPLC-18-DB (25 cm x 10 mm, 5 m) column with a gradient of thefollowing compounds. A) H20, B) K2HPO4 (pH 6.6), C) CH3OH. Set the gradient to 0 min,100% B; 5 min, 100% B; 14 min, 7% A/70% B/23% C; 25 min, 25% A/75% C; 28 min to34 min, 100% B.

steps.title: Construct a calibration curve with known...
steps.instruction: Construct a calibration curve with known concentrations of thiamin pyrophosphate (TPP),thiamin monophosphate (TMP), and free thiamin (ThF). Integrate the fluorescence signalpeak area. Quantification of thiamin quota

steps.title: Quantify ThF, TMP and TPP concentrations...
steps.instruction: Quantify ThF, TMP and TPP concentrations in the samples by plotting thefluorescence signal peak area on the HPLC chromatogram against the respectivestandard curve.

steps.title: Biosynthesis of thiamin I
steps.instruction: Biosynthesis of thiamin I: The function of the ThiEgene product. J A Chem Soc 117:2351-2352.Guillard, RRL.

steps.title: Culture of phytoplankton for feeding marine...
steps.instruction: Culture of phytoplankton for feeding marine invertebrates in “Culture ofMarine Invertebrate Animals.” (eds: Smith W.L. and Chanley M.H.) Plenum Press, New York,USA. 26-60.Pinto E, Pedersén M, Snoeijs P, Van Nieuwerburgh L, Colepicolo P.

steps.title: Simultaneousdetection of thiamine and its phosphate...
steps.instruction: Simultaneousdetection of thiamine and its phosphate esters from microalgae by HPLC. Biochem Biophys ResCommun 291:344–348.Reddick JJ, Nicewonger R, Begley TP.

steps.title: Mechanistic studies on thiamin phosphatesynthase
steps.instruction: Mechanistic studies on thiamin phosphatesynthase: Evidence for a dissociative mechanism. Biochem 40:10095-10102.Sylvander P, Häubner N, Snoeijs P.

steps.title: The thiamine content of phytoplankton cells...
steps.instruction: The thiamine content of phytoplankton cells is affectedby abiotic stress and growth rate. Microb Ecol 65:566–577.
